# Ambient Discoverability

> **Vocabulary update (per JOS-D0077, 2026-05-20):** the term `component`
> now replaces `governed object` / `resource` everywhere. See [JOS-C49]
> for the "parts on a shelf" framing that extends this concept. Body
> text below preserves the original 2026-04-20 framing; the cross-refs
> at the bottom link to the post-D0077 vocabulary.

## The Physics

The inventory inspires the solution. When people and agents can SEE what
capabilities, patterns, templates, and tools are available — without having
to know what to search for — new combinations emerge that nobody planned.
This is the difference between a filing cabinet (you must know what you want)
and an open kitchen (seeing ingredients triggers recipes).

Discoverability is not just about finding known things. It is about DISCOVERING
things you didn't know existed, and realizing you need them.

## Why It Matters

- **Innovation**: Visible capabilities suggest new combinations.
- **Onboarding**: New participants learn what exists by browsing, not by asking.
- **Reuse**: If people don't know a pattern exists, they reinvent it.
- **Governance compliance**: People follow governance they can see. They bypass governance they can't find.

## Academic/Industry Backing

- Open kitchen restaurant concept: transparency breeds trust and engagement.
- API developer portals: discoverability drives adoption.
- STD-08/STD-11 registry patterns: the mechanical implementation of ambient discoverability.
- Backstage (Spotify): internal developer portal as discoverability surface.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-P07 (Ambient Discoverability principle) — the principle enforcing this concept
- JOS-S01 (Discoverable Registries) — the standard for how discoverability works
- JOS-C01 (Fractal Self-Similarity) — discoverable patterns are fractal
- **JOS-C49 (Components as Parts on a Shelf)** — extends this concept with the "parts on a shelf" metaphor + canonical `component` vocabulary (per JOS-D0077, 2026-05-20)
- **JOS-D0077** — Components-as-Parts-on-a-Shelf decision (locks vocabulary + Registry/Index/Manifest distinction + single-source render model)
- **JOS-SPEC-021** — Registry / Index / Manifest schema spec

### Framework Connection

Framework §2 (lens navigation model) provides the mechanism through which discoverability becomes ambient — users anchor at any node and see L0-L3. §13 (four-layer identity/resolver) ensures every discoverable object has a stable, resolvable address.
